jokes.text: 'That's it! What is the point of the drink? I am missing the point.'
jokes.setup: Larry's mounting frustration with the drinks concept
jokes.punchline: His exasperated admission that he fundamentally doesn't understand social drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Larry's character - demanding logical explanations for basic social rituals.

jokes.text: 'Yeah, I did, but I decided to take the wrong food home. Thank you for inquiring.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks if he checked the order
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response implying obvious stupidity of the question
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry sarcasm - economical and perfectly delivered deadpan response to an obvious question.

jokes.text: Larry's elaborate lie about having a stepfather in an accident
jokes.setup: Larry explains his past deception to HBO
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity and Larry's matter-of-fact delivery about lying
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag while revealing Larry's casual relationship with deception.

jokes.text: 'I just thought it was a better lie. 'Cause if you make up a lie about a stepfather... you'll be believed, don't you think?'
jokes.setup: Cheryl questions why he lied about a stepfather
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted logic about what makes lies more believable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight into Larry's mind - he has a whole philosophy about effective lying that's completely absurd yet logically consistent.

jokes.text: The discovery of only three shrimp in the kung pao container
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl sit down to eat their Chinese food
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation that most of the shrimp are missing
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's main conflict. The mundane observation that launches the entire shrimp conspiracy.

jokes.text: Larry's immediate conclusion that Wasserman stole the shrimp
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately constructs an elaborate theory about HBO executive theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry behavior - immediately jumping to the most paranoid and accusatory conclusion possible about a minor inconvenience.

jokes.text: 'Did he take the shrimp?' 'I'll bet he took the shrimp.'
jokes.setup: Larry realizes Allan Wasserman had his food
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately jumps to conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry paranoia - his mind immediately goes to the worst possible explanation

jokes.text: 'No, we can go back and forth.' 'We don't need back and forth, we'll just do forth. Don't need back.'
jokes.setup: Julia offers to alternate meeting locations
jokes.punchline: Larry's literal interpretation of 'back and forth'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals Larry's overthinking of simple phrases

jokes.text: 'You've turned into a very nice man.' 'I'm a lovely gentleman.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-satisfied agreement, which will be immediately contradicted by his behavior
jokes.explanation: Beautiful ironic setup given Larry's upcoming behavior. The self-congratulation makes his later actions even funnier.

jokes.text: The 'Aren't You Evelyn?' TV show pitch
jokes.setup: Larry pitching a show about an actress typecast by a famous role
jokes.punchline: The obvious parallel to Julia's real-life Seinfeld experience
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on the actors' real situation. Self-aware without being too cute about it.

jokes.text: 'Really, this is all he can do, between you and me.'
jokes.setup: Julia suggesting Jason might be better for the role
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal honesty about Jason's limited acting range
jokes.explanation: Larry's characteristic brutal honesty about his former co-star. Cringe-worthy but true to character.

jokes.text: I want to be able to say 'fuck,' you know. And I wanna say... 'Cocksucker.' You don't need to say that. Actually, I do. I haven't really gotten to say that a lot.
jokes.setup: Julia wants creative freedom on HBO
jokes.punchline: Larry's childish excitement about saying forbidden words
jokes.explanation: Julia's professional desire for creative freedom vs Larry's juvenile fascination with curse words

jokes.text: I think he's bad, I'm not gonna go into why. I just don't think he's a good guy.
jokes.punchline: His vague character assassination based on shrimp theft suspicion
jokes.explanation: Larry's grudge over shrimp affects his professional judgment - classic character consistency

jokes.text: 'Gay guy?' 'Gay, no, he's not gay, he's married.' 'I could have sworn he was gay, that guy.'
jokes.punchline: Julia's confident but wrong assumption about his sexuality
jokes.explanation: Sets up the character dynamic and foreshadows later confusion. Builds the running gag about Michael's perceived sexuality.

jokes.text: She has a very high tolerance for pain, this woman, don't worry about it.
jokes.punchline: His casual assessment implies he regularly hurts his clients
jokes.explanation: Jojo's matter-of-fact delivery about pain tolerance suggests questionable massage techniques

jokes.text: Larry's drink argument retold to the HBO executives
jokes.setup: Larry trying to make small talk before the pitch
jokes.punchline: He rehashes his domestic argument with strangers in a business setting
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete inability to read the room. Turning personal grievances into business meeting small talk.

jokes.text: 'Maybe Cheryl wanted to get laid.'
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate sexual interpretation in a business setting
jokes.explanation: Escalates the inappropriateness of the conversation. Good cringe moment in professional setting.

jokes.text: The elaborate greeting ritual at HBO
jokes.punchline: Larry's commentary on the forced Hollywood socializing
jokes.explanation: Larry David's disdain for Hollywood phoniness. 'Gotta make the nice' is perfectly economical.

jokes.text: 'How's the stepfather doing?' 'Good.' 'He's doing better now?' 'Yeah.' 'Yeah, miraculous recovery he had?'
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable probing about the fictional stepfather
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of tension. Allan clearly knows Larry was lying, making this deliciously uncomfortable.

jokes.text: 'How were the garlic noodles, the other night?'
jokes.setup: About to pitch the TV show
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately derails with the shrimp accusation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - completely derailing an important meeting over a petty grievance. Economical and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Larry counting and documenting the missing shrimp in detail
jokes.setup: Confronting Allan about the food mix-up
jokes.punchline: Larry's obsessive documentation of the exact shrimp count
jokes.explanation: Larry's obsessive nature on full display. The specificity makes it funnier - he's not just mad, he's documented evidence.

jokes.text: 'You counted the shrimp, you're a shrimp-counter now.'
jokes.punchline: Allan's perfect dismissive characterization
jokes.explanation: 'Shrimp-counter' is a perfectly economical insult that captures Larry's obsessive behavior. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: 'If you want shrimp, take your $475 million, go buy a fucking shrimp boat.'
jokes.punchline: Allan's nuclear response suggesting Larry buy his own shrimp boat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation. The specificity of '$475 million' and 'shrimp boat' makes it both absurd and memorable. Peak comedy writing.

jokes.text: 'Nothing changes in 25 years with this guy. Same shit.'
jokes.punchline: His parting shot about Larry's unchanging personality
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - suggests Larry has always been this way. Adds depth to the relationship history.

jokes.text: 'He implied that I was lying about my stepfather.' 'You don't have a stepfather.' 'But I didn't like the implication.'
jokes.punchline: His indignation about being called out on a lie he admits is a lie
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David logic - being offended about someone questioning a lie you openly admit to telling. Absurd but perfectly character-consistent.

jokes.text: 'The implication for your imaginary stepfather? How can he insult you about somebody who's imaginary?'
jokes.setup: Larry's offense at the stepfather implication
jokes.punchline: Julia's logical breakdown of the impossibility of being insulted about fictional people
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response highlighting the absurdity of Larry's position. Good logical comedy.

jokes.text: 'Larry, everybody steals shrimp, and everybody lies about it. This is Hollywood.'
jokes.setup: Larry's outrage about the shrimp theft
jokes.punchline: Julia's matter-of-fact acceptance of Hollywood corruption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant summation of Hollywood morality. The specificity of 'shrimp' makes a broader point about corruption. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: 'You know, my dentist told me I have the gums of a 25-year-old man. I think it was about the nicest thing anyone's ever said to me.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's pathetic pride in his gum health compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment revealing Larry's low self-esteem and desperate need for validation. Sad and funny.

jokes.text: 'I guess when they switched them back, Allan had taken seven of your shrimp?' 'That's my claim, yeah.' 'Allegedly.'
jokes.setup: Michael wants to hear about the shrimp incident
jokes.punchline: Larry's legal-sounding 'allegedly' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that treats the shrimp thing like a legal case

jokes.text: 'I saw the Jackson Pollock movie the night before... and I got very influenced by the artist, you know.'
jokes.punchline: Blaming his tantrum on art film inspiration
jokes.explanation: Absurd excuse for bad behavior. The idea that watching a biopic makes you dramatically storm out of meetings is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: 'Because GE will not take responsibility for dumping the PCBs in the Hudson river.'
jokes.setup: Asked why they didn't go to NBC
jokes.punchline: Julia's environmental activism as show business reasoning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Julia's principles. Absurd but admirable mixing of environmental concerns with career decisions.

jokes.text: 'So you throw in a "fuck," you double your laughs. It's a crowd-pleaser. "Fuck" is a crowd-pleaser.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical analysis of profanity as comedy crutch
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on comedy writing. Larry analyzing the mechanical aspects of getting laughs.

jokes.text: 'You cunt, what a cunt!'
jokes.setup: Michael not going in with ace high
jokes.punchline: Larry's explosive inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: The moment that destroys everything. Perfect economy and massive impact. Larry's complete social blindness on display.

jokes.text: 'I think that's kind of a misogynist thing to say.'
jokes.punchline: Julia's immediate feminist response
jokes.explanation: Escalates the situation. Julia correctly identifies the problem but Larry can't see it.

jokes.text: 'That kind of language is not allowed in a game... in a decent game.'
jokes.punchline: Brad's moral authority about decent games
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the social consequences. Everyone turns against Larry.

jokes.text: 'It's a word you use when somebody doesn't act manly. And he didn't act manly, he should've gone in.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted justification for the slur
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - completely missing why it's offensive and doubling down with terrible logic.

jokes.text: 'Maybe in retrospect, I should've said, "Pussy."'
jokes.punchline: Larry's 'better' choice that's equally offensive
jokes.explanation: Beautiful escalation showing Larry's complete tone-deafness. He thinks he's being more considerate.

jokes.text: Maybe in retrospect, I should've said, 'Pussy.'
jokes.setup: Larry reflecting on his word choice
jokes.punchline: His 'better' alternative is equally offensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David logic - his attempted correction makes everything worse

jokes.text: 'Maybe I am a misogynist. Maybe I like to beat my wife a little bit.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggesting he might be misogynist
jokes.punchline: Larry's darkly comic escalation to wife-beating
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that sets up the episode's final plot thread. Larry joking about something that will be taken seriously.

jokes.text: 'I can't believe that guy called me a misogynist.' 'Nobody ever called me that before.' 'Maybe you are.'
jokes.setup: Larry is shocked by the accusation
jokes.punchline: Cheryl suggests it might be true
jokes.explanation: Solid marital comedy - wife's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Larry playfully hitting Cheryl in the car
jokes.punchline: The physical comedy that will be catastrophically misinterpreted
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the misunderstanding. Innocent behavior that looks terrible out of context.

jokes.text: 'Big deal, I call men pricks all the time, men want to work with me.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's false equivalency between gendered slurs
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete inability to understand why one word is worse than another. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: 'Cunt's worse.' 'It's not worse, pricks and cunts, they're equal.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's mathematical approach to offensive language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - trying to create mathematical equality between gendered slurs. 'They balance out' is perfectly economical.

jokes.text: 'Michael Halbreich took a leave of absence... he's shell-shocked... and he's examining everything in his life.'
jokes.punchline: The dramatic life crisis caused by being called a name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing the massive consequences of Larry's thoughtlessness. Perfectly absurd response.

jokes.text: 'I thought he was one of those effeminate heterosexuals.' 'There's no such thing as an effeminate heterosexual.'
jokes.punchline: Julia's definitive statement about sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier confusion. Larry's category creation vs Julia's black-and-white view.

jokes.text: 'How many shrimp do you generally put in the kung pao shrimp?' 'Eleven.' 'You always put in 11?' 'We have a very strict policy about that.'
jokes.punchline: Confirmation that Allan definitely stole shrimp
jokes.explanation: Perfect vindication of Larry's obsessiveness. The 'strict policy' makes Allan's theft undeniable. Great payoff.

jokes.text: Mickey the dentist witnessing the car 'abuse' and misinterpreting it
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl's playful car routine
jokes.punchline: The dentist's horrified misinterpretation of domestic violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation. Innocent behavior becomes evidence of serious abuse through misinterpretation.

jokes.text: 'I have seen Larry David in action, and that man is an animal... and he has to be stopped.'
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration treating Larry like a dangerous criminal
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the misunderstanding. Mickey treating Larry like a public menace is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: 'Dentist got in touch with Wasserman, thinks I'm a wife-beater.'
jokes.punchline: The final nail in Larry's HBO coffin
jokes.explanation: Perfect convergence of all the episode's disasters. Larry's reputation completely destroyed through misunderstandings.

jokes.text: The dinner-to-dessert venue argument recreating the opening scene
jokes.punchline: Larry and Cheryl repeating their fundamental incompatibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect structural callback showing nothing has changed. The circular nature of their relationship conflicts.
